title: ophioglossum minimum
intro: Ophioglossum minimum, also known as the dwarf adder's tongue fern, is a rare, terrestrial, and deciduous fern species native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that can be used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Ophioglossum minimum include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate rheumatism, and even treat snake bites, making it a valuable resource in natural remedies. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations such as tea infusions, tinctures, and infusions of the roots, which are said to enhance its therapeutic properties.
